title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After being locked in a musty room by Mosgolovsky, Eileen Bond is led by Mosgrove to an emergency meeting of the Seven Clocks Association. Inside the secret room, Bond finds masked individuals seated around a table. The figure at the head of the table, wearing a seven o'clock mask, reveals himself to be Detective Bartholomew. 

Bartholomew explains that the Seven Clocks Association is not a criminal organization, but a group of amateur spies founded by the late Gerry Wade to conduct secret intelligence work. He identifies the members: Mosgolovsky serves as his second-in-command; Count Andras of Hungary is five o'clock; American journalist Hayward Felphs is four o'clock; and Bill Everly is three o'clock. The two o'clock seat is vacant due to the death of Ronnie DeVille, and the one o'clock position is held by Countess Razky, who is revealed to be a top-notch actress named Barbara Saint-Moore.

Bartholomew explains that the group's purpose was to catch a dangerous thief capable of stealing secret inventions. This thief, Jimmy Sessage, was eventually caught by Bill Everly. Bartholomew clarifies that while Gerry Wade's death appeared to be a sleeping pill overdose, Mr. De Vere believed Wade was murdered by a skilled killer within their social circle. De Vere had suspected Sessage but had not yet revealed his suspicions before his own death.
